description: Expression or conduct is 'noemi' when it is faithful to a person's own commitments and lived character rather than performed to imitate an expected image; creative work may carry the same quality. The word does not claim that a person has one fixed hidden essence, and people may change while remaining authentic. 'thaemu' (sincere) concerns whether a present expression matches actual feeling or intention. 'shewo' (true) concerns reality or genuineness.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and open into rounded true-mid back 'o'. Release the rounding and move the tongue forward for stressed true-mid 'e' in its own syllable. Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', then raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The dental and bilabial nasals hold the vowels at either side of the stressed hiatus. As 'o.e.i' moves forward and unrounds, the word seems to bring a voice gradually into view.
usage_notes: The quality-noun rule supplies authenticity. 'noemi' often accompanies 'haoni' (voice), conduct, or creative work; the relevant commitment, practice, or tradition may appear in context when it matters.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Within a non-self account, authenticity cannot depend on a permanent hidden essence. 'noemi' stays with commitments and conduct that a changing person still recognizes as their own.

semantic_domains.communication: a voice or expression faithful to the speaker's lived commitments
semantic_domains.ethics: fidelity between a person's conduct and the commitments they recognize as their own
